title: Sanitize target_files input in meeseeks.py
source: Codebase audit — meeseeks.py target_files passed unsanitized into prompts
description: In meeseeks.py, the target_files list from args is embedded directly into the prompt without any validation or sanitization. An attacker could inject path traversal payloads (../../etc/passwd) or prompt injection content disguised as filenames. This is a high-risk gap since meeseeks is designed for quick ad-hoc tasks with less oversight.
proposed_changes: 1. Import validate_safe_path and sanitize_path_component from security_utils. 2. Before building the prompt, validate each file in target_files: reject entries containing '..', null bytes, or absolute paths outside the project root. Use validate_safe_path(f, base_dir=project_root) for each file. 3. Wrap the files_text block with wrap_untrusted_content(files_text, label='TARGET_FILES') before embedding in the prompt. 4. Add a max file count limit (e.g., 20) to prevent prompt stuffing attacks.
